titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Frank Schilling Cummings Papers
titlestmt.author: Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 15962822.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 00:33

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English. Some sound recordings in Navajo and Inuit (likely Inupiaq).

did.abstract: Photographs, audiovisual material, and papers of Frank Schilling Cummings relating to the Navajo peoples near Monument Valley, Arizona, circa 1940s-1970s. 1,010 photographs and slides depict Navajo men, women, and children, as well as Monument Valley. 49 reel-to-reel audio tapes contain oral histories, language lessons, and chants, songs, and ceremonies by Navajo peoples. Two additional tapes pertain to the Aleut peoples of Alaska, while a third contains an informal language lesson in "Eskimo" (likely Inupiaq). Included are two VHS tapes, one 16mm film reel, one DVD, and one external hard drive.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection contains potentially culturally sensitive material related to the Navaho Nation. As of February 2024, it is closed to research pending consultation with tribal representatives.
Boxes 5-17 (audiovisual materials) and Box 18 (born digital material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Public Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Langdon Manor Books, LLC on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Photographs, circa 1940s-1970s. II. Audiovisual Material, 1952-1973. III. Computer Media, circa 2017. IV. Papers, 1945-1973.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Frank Schilling Cummings (1894-1978) was an engineer and amateur anthropologist who grew up in Los Angeles, California, graduated Los Angeles Polytechnic High School in 1912, and settled in Oakland after attending college in New Jersey. In 1949, Cummings became acquainted with Frank Todacheene, a Navajo man. From this relationship, Frank Cummings and wife Betty Cummings photographed and recorded the traditions of the Navajo peoples near Monument Valley, Arizona, for more than two decades.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Frank Schilling Cummings Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, audiovisual material, and papers of Frank Schilling Cummings relating to the Navajo peoples near Monument Valley, Arizona, circa 1940s-1970s. 1,010 photographs and slides depict Navajo men, women, and children, as well as Monument Valley. 49 reel-to-reel audio tapes contain oral histories, language lessons, and chants, songs, and ceremonies by Navajo peoples. Two additional tapes pertain to the Aleut peoples of Alaska, while a third contains an informal language lesson in "Eskimo" (likely Inupiaq). Included are two VHS tapes, one 16mm film reel, one DVD, and one external hard drive.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Frank Schilling Cummings Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Material
relatedmaterial.p: Frank Schilling Cummings Papers (WH497), Denver Public Library, Denver, Colorado.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Material
relatedmaterial.p: Frank Schilling Cummings Film Collection (MS-305), Museum of Northern Arizona, Flagstaff, Arizona.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by format.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains 1,010 photographs and slides of Navajo men, women, and children, and Monument Valley, Arizona.
Some slides appear to document family vacations taken by Frank and Betty Cummings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged alphabetically by format.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 49 reel-to-reel audio tapes contain oral histories, language lessons, and chants, songs, and ceremonies by Navajo peoples. Two additional tapes pertain to the Aleut peoples of Alaska, while a third contains an informal language lesson in "Eskimo" (likely Inupiaq). Included are two VHS tapes and a 16mm film titled "Sun City Lake."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Public Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Maurice Knee, John Watchman & Flora - History
John Watchman, Phillip Jackson - Bear Song Oklahoma Ceremonies

Monument Valley 1954, Ruins Rangers etc. 1957, Navajo water 1959, M.V. sand painting 1949

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Public Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged alphabetically by format.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains a film index of Frank S. Cummings's films (not present in collection), five notebooks recording brief notes made during multiple trips, Cummings's personal and family history, and other papers.
